summary: I addressed feedback item F1 by adding `attempt: 2` to the three offending `step_runs.create!` calls in `test/services/phases/restart_define_test.rb` (the "refuses to restart while a step is active", "does not carry non-human feedback", and "carries prior human-tagged feedback onto the seeded run" tests). Each of these tests seeds an extra step_run for `@requirements` at `iteration: 1`, which without an explicit `attempt` defaulted to `1` and collided with the `requirements_ready` fixture already sitting at `(step: requirements_writer, iteration: 1, attempt: 1)`, causing `ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique`. Setting `attempt: 2` gives each seeded run a unique `(step_id, iteration, attempt)` tuple while preserving the tests' intent (feedback carry-over and busy-state detection don't depend on the attempt number). I couldn't execute `bin/rails test` in this environment because the worktree's bundle is missing several gems (bootsnap, tailwindcss-ruby, bcrypt, etc.) — a pre-existing environment issue unrelated to this change, reproducible even in the main checkout — so I verified the fix with `ruby -c` syntax checking instead. No other files were modified.
